description_long: This object is a long, traditional-style rifle with a wooden stock and a lengthy, octagonal metal barrel. The stock is crafted from polished wood, featuring a brass or gold-toned buttplate and accents, giving it a classic, antique appearance. The barrel is metallic, likely steel, and extends significantly from the stock, suggesting it is a muzzle-loading or early cartridge rifle. The trigger guard and hammer mechanism are visible, indicating a single-shot, manually operated action. The rifle lacks any modern attachments, with only basic iron sights present atop the barrel. The overall design is reminiscent of 18th or 19th-century hunting or military rifles, with a focus on craftsmanship and durability.
description: A long, antique-style rifle with a polished wooden stock, brass accents, and an extended steel barrel. Features classic iron sights and a single-shot action.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, highlighting the wooden stock, brass buttplate, and long steel barrel.
description_view_1: Front view down the barrel, emphasizing the octagonal shape and the alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the same features as view 0 but from the other side, with the trigger and hammer mechanism visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the brass buttplate and the wooden stock's end, with the barrel extending away.
